summary: Iteration 2 testing of follow-up features (patient profile, doctor My Patients, admin appointments mgmt, Firebase password reset, image upload). Backend: 32/32 pytest pass (all new routes properly guard auth; public /api/files/{path} correctly bypasses auth and returns 404 for missing objects). Frontend: Patient Profile page /patient/profile works end-to-end (save + reload persistence verified); 'Profile' nav link present; Forgot-password dialog opens and submits without JS crash. BLOCKER: AdminDashboard.jsx crashes with ReferenceError — entire /admin page unusable, so Admin Appointments tab / Reschedule / Cancel could not be exercised in the UI.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught ReferenceError: CalendarDays is not defined. Admin console fails to render. Source review shows MANY missing imports/decls: (1) lucide-react icons `CalendarDays`, `Edit`, `Ban`; (2) shadcn `Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle`; (3) `Input`, `Label`; (4) `resolveImageUrl` from @/lib/utils; (5) state `const [appointments, setAppointments] = useState([])` and `const [editing, setEditing] = useState(null)` are used but never declared; (6) `loadAll` never fetches /api/admin/appointments so the tab would be empty even if imports were fixed. All admin tabs (pending doctors, doctors, users, high-risk, appointments) are blocked because the component throws before its first render.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be reached in UI due to AdminDashboard render crash; backend PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/appointments/{id} are present and guard-correct (401 unauth) but frontend cannot call them.

action_items: Fix /app/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminDashboard.jsx: add imports `CalendarDays, Edit, Ban` from lucide-react; `Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle` from @/components/ui/dialog; `Input` from @/components/ui/input; `Label` from @/components/ui/label; `resolveImageUrl` from @/lib/utils; declare `const [appointments, setAppointments] = useState([])` and `const [editing, setEditing] = useState(null)`; extend `loadAll` to `api.get('/admin/appointments')` and call `setAppointments(res.data)`.

After fix, re-test admin flow: open /admin, click Appointments tab, click Reschedule on an appointment to open dialog with `[data-testid=reschedule-date]` + `[data-testid=reschedule-slot]`, save, and verify Cancel action flips status to 'cancelled' and emits [MOCKED EMAIL] line in /var/log/supervisor/backend.out.log.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminDashboard.jsx has multiple undefined references (see ui_bugs) — was never rendered before committing. Add a smoke test that mounts each top-level route.
AdminDashboard uses `setEditing` inside `rescheduleAppt` but the state hook was forgotten; suggests copy-paste from a sketch that was never compiled-checked.
The useEffect at line 247 references `appt` only, but `RescheduleDialog` fine-locally manages `date` / `slot` — OK once imports exist.
notification_service.send_appointment_booked is imported nowhere we could verify in appointment.py in this review. Main agent should confirm it's actually called on POST /api/appointments/.
upload.py's download_file raises HTTPException from bare Exception — acceptable but masks real storage errors; consider logging resp.status_code.

storage_service.init_storage() does a synchronous network POST on first use inside an async endpoint — could block the event loop. Consider running via run_in_threadpool or caching the key at startup.

success_rate.frontend: ~70% (patient profile + forgot-password verified; admin dashboard blocker; doctor MyPatients & ImageUpload button not UI-tested due to time but source looks correct)

test_credentials: Created fresh during this run: patient e2e_patient_1776796883@aura-test.dev / Patient123! (has profile: age=29 female 'anxiety, sleep issues'); admin e2e_admin_<ts>@aura-test.dev / Admin123! (role set but admin UI crashes). Previously-approved doctor id=1 still in SQLite.
seed_data_creation: One patient profile row created in SQLite via POST /api/patient/profile for the freshly-signed-up patient above.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AdminDashboard.jsx is the ONLY blocker. Once main agent applies the fix (imports + useState + loadAll fetch), the next testing agent should: (1) sign in as admin, (2) open /admin and click tab-appointments, (3) find a booked appointment (booked by the seeded patient against doctor id=1 — if none exists, first book via the patient flow), (4) verify Reschedule dialog saves new date/slot and Cancel flips status and logs [MOCKED EMAIL] in /var/log/supervisor/backend.out.log. Patient Profile + Forgot-password are already confirmed green. Backend is fully green and does not need re-testing unless main agent changes server.py.
rca of the issue: AdminDashboard.jsx was committed without compile/render verification. Webpack only flagged a transient template-literal syntax error (fixed) but did NOT flag the runtime ReferenceErrors (CalendarDays, Edit, Ban, Dialog*, Input, Label, resolveImageUrl) because webpack treats undeclared identifiers as 'globals' at bundle-time. The component throws on first render. Reproduction: sign in as admin → navigate to /admin → red 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay with 'CalendarDays is not defined'. Mitigation: add the missing imports and declare the two missing useState hooks, then also wire `/admin/appointments` into loadAll.
